Camp 8 to Matun-og Falls is a documented out-and-back community trail in Minglanilla. The route descends into a valley through farms, mountain viewpoints, and local settlements before continuing toward Matun-og Falls. Although the distance is relatively short at approximately 6.2 km, the continuous climbs and descents require good endurance and careful navigation.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
TOUGH<\/div>
<\/div><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>July 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>6.2 km (Out and Back)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>457 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>4-5 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 2 liters<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>100% trail<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Wikiloc<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>

Forecast data: Open-Meteo<\/a><\/p>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began from Camp 8, Minglanilla via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the road near the jump-off point, with no parking fee observed during the hike. Private vehicles were able to access the trailhead, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Tough<\/h3>
\u2713 Significant endurance required<\/div>
\u2713 Sustained steep ascent or descent<\/div>
\u2713 Limited or no mobile signal<\/div>
\u2713 Navigation requires attention<\/div>
\u2713 Limited bailout options<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> farmland<\/a> \u2022<\/span> river crossing<\/a> \u2022<\/span> valley<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route follows a mostly straightforward community trail with steady climbs and descents through farmland, open mountain scenery, and local settlements. Several sections provide mountain viewpoints, while frequent encounters with local residents are common along the way.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Although the trail is not isolated, some portions feel more secluded because of dense vegetation. Navigation requires attention, as multiple paths branch off toward different houses and destinations.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike did not reach Matun-og Falls. The hike was turned back before the destination due to bad weather, dense vegetation, and a river crossing. Although there was no heavy downpour, the decision to return was made as a precaution.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy with occasional rain. The trail was dry at the time but was observed to become very muddy and slippery after rainfall. Shade coverage was limited, and no stores were encountered along the route. Mobile signal was strong near the start before disappearing farther into the trail.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

At least 2 liters of water was recommended for this hike. Many insects were present, and the only people encountered during the hike were local residents.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

A trekking pole or walking stick is highly recommended because of the continuous ascents and descents. It may also be useful when encountering dogs along the route.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Farm animals, including carabaos and cows, were commonly encountered. When necessary, briefly leaving the GPX track to maintain a safe distance while passing was noted as a practical option.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
